recipient_and_agency_information.county_.answer: Office of the County Clerk-Recorder, 600 8th Street

recipient_and_agency_information.public_.answer: City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

project_information.project_title.answer: 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project

project_information.project_applicant.answer: City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

project_information.project_location_spe.answer: 14th Avenue between Stockton Boulevard and 65th Street, Sacramento, CA 95820 (APNs 021-0450-012, 021-0450-013, 021-0460-001, and adjacent public right-of-way)

project_information.description_of_natur.answer: The 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project will retrofit an existing approximately 1.2-mile segment of 14th Avenue, an established urban collector roadway, to improve pedestrian, bicycle, and traffic safety while maintaining existing vehicular capacity. The project includes installation of new ADA-compliant curb ramps at all intersections, high-visibility crosswalks, upgraded pedestrian signal heads with countdown timers, Class II buffered bike lanes in both directions achieved primarily through restriping within existing curb-to-curb width, and limited sidewalk infill where short gaps currently force pedestrians into the roadway.

Additional elements include replacement of deteriorated pavement sections, minor drainage inlet adjustments, new street lighting at selected intersections to improve nighttime visibility, and installation of traffic-calming features such as curb extensions (bulb-outs) at key crosswalks adjacent to schools and neighborhood parks. All work is confined to previously disturbed and developed public right-of-way; no acquisition of new right-of-way is required, and no expansion of the roadway footprint beyond existing curbs will occur. Construction activities will be phased to maintain access to residences, local businesses, and emergency services at all times.

The primary beneficiaries of the project are local residents of the Tahoe Park and Tallac Village neighborhoods, students and families walking and biking to nearby schools (including Will C. Wood Middle School and Tahoe Elementary School), transit riders accessing Sacramento Regional Transit bus routes that run along or cross 14th Avenue, and motorists traveling between Stockton Boulevard and 65th Street. By reducing vehicle speeds, shortening pedestrian crossing distances, and providing dedicated bicycle facilities, the project is intended to reduce the frequency and severity of collisions, encourage active transportation, and improve safe access to community resources such as parks, schools, small businesses, and medical offices along the corridor.

exemption_determination.name_of_person_o.answer: City of Sacramento Department of Public Works, Engineering and Project Delivery Division

exemption_determination.categorical_exem.answer: Categorical Exemption, Class 1 – Existing Facilities (CEQA Guidelines §15301(c))

exemption_determination.reasons_why_proj.answer: The project consists of minor alterations to existing public streets, sidewalks, and related appurtenances involving negligible or no expansion of use beyond that existing at the time of the lead agency’s determination. All proposed improvements—including pavement rehabilitation, restriping to add buffered bike lanes within existing curb lines, installation of ADA-compliant curb ramps, crosswalk enhancements, minor drainage modifications, and lighting upgrades—occur entirely within the existing, fully improved public right-of-way along 14th Avenue. As such, the project is categorically exempt from CEQA under Class 1 (Existing Facilities), CEQA Guidelines §15301(c), which specifically includes existing streets, sidewalks, bicycle and pedestrian trails, and similar facilities.

The project area is fully urbanized, previously graded, and developed, and does not contain sensitive biological resources, prime farmland, wetlands, or designated scenic resources. The improvements do not increase the number of traffic lanes, do not induce substantial growth, and do not involve any unusual circumstances that would create a reasonable possibility of a significant effect on the environment. There are no known hazardous waste sites on or adjacent to the project corridor as listed pursuant to Government Code §65962.5, and the project will comply with all applicable City standard construction specifications, noise control ordinances, and best management practices for dust and stormwater. Therefore, none of the exceptions to the use of categorical exemptions set forth in CEQA Guidelines §15300.2 apply, and the Class 1 exemption is appropriate.

lead_agency_contact.lead_agency.answer: City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

applicant_filing_and_certification.title.answer: Senior Planner, Environmental Services, Department of Public Works
